title: RecommendationObject
description: Structured output of core's N-model AI panel, returned by getRecommendation(intakeAnswers, config). This is the single contract every front-end (bot/, instanthpi/, epic/) consumes -- none of them talk to AI providers directly. Independent models in the panel must reach consensus; material disagreement is surfaced via divergenceFlag rather than silently resolved.

considerations.description: Ranked list of clinical possibilities/considerations the panel discussed. This is deliberately framed as 'considerations', not a diagnosis -- front-ends must not relabel it as one.

text.description: Plain-language statement of the consideration, e.g. 'Could be consistent with a viral upper respiratory infection.'

confidence.description: Panel's aggregate confidence in this consideration, 0 (least) to 1 (most). Reflects agreement across panel members, not certainty of diagnosis.

divergenceFlag.description: True if the AI panel members disagreed materially (e.g. conflicting red-flag calls, or considerations that don't overlap at all). When true, front-ends SHOULD surface this to the user/physician explicitly rather than silently presenting a single blended answer -- this flag is the substitute for physician oversight in deployments with no reviewing clinician.

redFlags.description: Urgent findings raised by the intake answers that warrant seeking care sooner rather than later, stated in plain language. Empty array means the panel identified no urgent findings -- it does NOT mean the situation is guaranteed benign.
examples: Chest pain spreading to the arm or jaw

suggestedNextSteps.description: Concrete, actionable next steps calibrated for someone who may have no doctor reachable nearby -- NOT a generic 'see a doctor' list. Each entry should be one self-contained instruction: what self-care to try at home, what specific change would mean it's time to travel to the nearest clinic/hospital, what to bring or say if they do travel, or how to safely monitor over the next hours/days. Order matters: least-invasive/most-immediately-actionable steps first.
examples: Drink small sips of clean water or oral rehydration solution every 15 minutes to prevent dehydration.
Keep track of how many times vomiting or diarrhea occurs in the next 6 hours.
If unable to keep any fluids down for more than 8 hours, or if you see blood, travel to the nearest clinic or hospital as soon as possible.
If a clinic is more than a few hours away and symptoms are worsening, do not wait for them to become severe before starting the journey.

billingSuggestions.description: Jurisdiction-aware billing code suggestions for the treating clinician's own records (e.g. RAMQ codes for Quebec, CPT-style generic codes elsewhere). Optional and nullable -- the patient-facing bot front-end will typically omit or ignore this entirely; only physician-facing front-ends (instanthpi/, epic/) are expected to use it. Never presented to the patient.

code.description: The billing code itself, in whatever format the jurisdiction uses.

description.description: Plain-language description of what the code covers.

jurisdiction.description: Which billing scheme this code belongs to, e.g. a RAMQ (Quebec) code, or a generic CPT-style code for jurisdictions without a specified public scheme.

plainLanguageSummary.description: A short, plain-language summary of the whole recommendation suitable for direct display to the patient/user, written at a level that assumes no medical background and no reliable follow-up access to a clinician.

panelMeta.description: Optional diagnostic metadata about how the panel produced this recommendation. Not required for front-ends to render, but useful for logging/debugging self-hosted deployments.

providersConsulted.description: Names/ids of the AI providers that participated in this panel run.

panelSize.description: How many independent model responses were aggregated into this recommendation.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the recommendation was generated.
